- Use the examples to infer discriminative visual patterns for each class.
- Do not use external world knowledge, hidden assumptions, or speculative attributes.

- Choose exactly one final label.

- Do not output any text outside the XML tags.

- The final line of the entire answer must be <response>final_class</response>.
- Never omit the final <response> block, even when another explanation block is required.

</explanation>

<explanation>
The Query Image shows a blurry, earth-toned creature with a rounded body and visible limbs, consistent with the visual characteristics of the frog examples (class 6) provided in the labeled examples. The shape and context (ground/soil) align with the frog class, not the airplane (class 0) or horse (class 7) examples.
</explanation>

<response>6</response>

<explanation>

</explanation>

<response>7</response>
